Order To Show Cause Why Preliminary Injunction Should Not Be Issued
Off Calendar May-07-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on the matter, the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows:
Order To Show Cause Why Preliminary Injunction Should Not Be Issued is taken Off calendar based on the representations from counsel for defendants that there are no remaining issues in dispute. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Ann Delgado, CSR # 11185

Order To Show Cause Why Preliminary Injunction Should Not Be Issued
Off Calendar Apr-16-2015 Continued To May-07-15 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. FERNANDO FLORES of the Department of Industrial Relations, Tel No. (415) 703-4814 appearing for Defendant Labor Comissioner, State of California, Department of Industrial Relations, Division of Labor Standards Enforcement;
No other appearances made in this matter.

This matter was not argued. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Order To Show Cause Why Preliminary Injunction Should Not Be Issued is continued to May 7, 2015 to permit the parties to make further efforts to resolve any remaining differences. If the parties have not fully resolved all issues in dispute before then, no later than May 4, 2015 they should, jointly or separately, file a brief statement of issues remaining in dispute. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Denise Doucette, CSR #5963 (302/HK)
